hypothesis: Tighten the verifiable-fact lede constraint: lede must be Northern Ontario-specific (St. Marys River, Lake Superior, Algoma district geography/weather/season) — not general industry statistics. Evidence: exp_1777002297 kept at 9/10 but blogs 3 and 4 used industry facts (50-hour oil change interval, spark plug failure rate) rather than local facts — these ledes are noticeably weaker than the Lake Superior ice-out and walleye season openers. Hypothesis: enforcing the Northern Ontario-specific requirement on every lede will produce consistent 9-10/10 drafts with no weak generic openers. Secondary change: vary lede sentence structure (measurement, date, geographic scale, seasonal event) so patterns don't feel formulaic across a batch.
learning: NULL/gate-blocked outcome. No content produced under this surface during the 48h window — the service-page standby directive (boss msg 1777135075295, Apr 25) held all content work. The tightened Northern Ontario-specific lede constraint is sound in theory (validated by exp_3 evidence: SSM ice-out + walleye season ledes scored higher than industry-fact ledes) but could not be measured because no output was produced. This is the first null in this cycle. Per autoresearch gate-dependent metric rule: escalate to analyst after 3-4 consecutive nulls. Not auto-creating exp_5 — waiting for gate to lift (service-page redirect) before the next experiment can produce measurable output.
changes_description: Tightened lede constraint in current.md: lede fact must be Northern Ontario-specific (geography, water systems, season dates, weather) — general industry statistics explicitly excluded. Added structure-variation rule to prevent formulaic ledes across a batch. Added confirmed staff roster note. All other rules carried forward from exp_3.
